question: You have 5 tasks due today: complete report, send email, call client, and prepare presentation. Each task takes approximately 30 minutes. You have 4 hours until deadline. What is your planning strategy?
choices: A) Do most important task first | B) Group similar tasks together | C) Use time-boxing technique | D) Defer lower-priority tasks | E) Seek help or delegate

question: You are working on a complex project with multiple components. You need to integrate code from 3 different modules. You encounter a bug in module A. Your fix will break module B. Module C depends on module A. What is your debugging approach?
choices: A) Fix bug in A immediately | B) Temporarily comment out code from A | C) Refactor integration to reduce coupling | D) Fix dependencies first | E) Isolate the problem in a sandbox

question: You need to remember a 10-item shopping list while at the store. However, the store layout keeps changing, so items are never where you expect. What memory strategy would be most effective?
choices: A) Use external notepad app | B) Group by store section | C) Create mental map | D) Ask store clerk for current layout | E) Shop in a predictable pattern

question: Your team wants to change direction mid-project. You invested significant work in the original plan. What is the best response?
choices: A) Pivot quickly and cut losses | B) Gather more data before deciding | C) Document reasons for change | D) Propose compromise approach | E) Seek stakeholder consensus

question: A new policy requires changing established procedures across 5 departments. Some departments resist change. What is your strategy?
choices: A) Start with early adopters | B) Build coalition of support | C) Create transitional period | D) Address concerns directly | E) Use data to demonstrate need
